dictionary_sections.definition: 153,600-DIMENSIONAL TRAJECTORY ENCODING is a source-backed registry entry whose production form is anchored to the reviewed equation G_\text{toroid}(r) = A \, r^n \, e^{-\frac{r^2}{2\sigma^2}}. The entry records the mathematical object, condition, or operator named by the title and prepares it for the locked TZPID four-page layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In context, 153,600-DIMENSIONAL TRAJECTORY ENCODING functions as a local mathematical checkpoint. The canonical equation gives the entry its printable formal spine, while the supporting equations show how the relation is used or constrained inside the surrounding registry arc.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The displayed relation permits the entry to be read as a concrete source-grounded step rather than a registry shell.
